cards.back: The number of bits used to represent a key, which determines the level of security it provides
The amount of time a key is valid or can be used before it needs to be updated or replaced
The process of creating a new key, typically using a key generation algorithm
The process of securely exchanging keys between parties, often using protocols like Diffie-Hellman or RSA
The process of safely storing keys, often using secure key stores or hardware security modules
